content:
# 1 Corinthians Chapter 11 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/1CO_011_page_001.jpg

1. Become imitators of me, just as I also [am] of Christos.

2. And I praise you that in all [things] you remember me and hold firmly to the traditions just as I delivered [them] to you.

3. And I want you to know that the head of every man is the Christos, and [the] head of [the] woman [is] the man, and [the] head of the Christos [is] God.

4. Every man praying or prophesying, having [something] down from [the] head, shames the head of him.

5. And every woman praying or prophesying with the head uncovered shames the head of her; for it is one and the same as having been shaved.

6. For if a woman does not cover herself, let her also be shorn; but if [it is] shameful for a woman to be shorn or shaved, let her cover herself.

7. For a man indeed ought not to cover the head, being [the] image and glory of God; but the woman is [the] glory of [the] man.

8. For man is not from woman, but woman from man.

9. For indeed man was not created on account of the woman, but woman on account of the man.

10. On account of this the woman ought to have authority upon the head, on account of the angels.

11. Nevertheless, neither [is] woman apart from man nor man apart from woman in [the] Lord.

12. For just as the woman [is] from the man, so also the man [is] through the woman; and all [things are] from God.

13. Judge among yourselves: is it proper for a woman to pray to God uncovered?

14. Does not nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a dishonor to him,

15. but if a woman has long hair, it is a glory to her? Because the long hair has been given to her as a covering.

16. But if anyone seems to be contentious, we have no such custom, nor the assemblies of God.

17. And in directing this, I do not praise [you], because you come together not for the better but for the worse.

18. For first indeed, when you come together in [the] assembly, I hear [that] divisions exist among you, and in part I believe [it].

19. For there must also be factions among you, that the approved [ones] may become evident among you.

20. Therefore when you come together upon the same [place], it is not to eat [the] Lord's supper;

21. for each takes his own supper first in the eating, and one is hungry and another is drunk.

22. For do you not have houses in which to eat and to drink? Or do you despise the assembly of God and shame the [ones] not having? What shall I say to you? Shall I praise you? In this I do not praise [you].

23. For I received from the Lord what I also delivered to you, that the Lord Yeshua, in the night in which he was delivered up, took bread,

24. and having given thanks, broke [it] and said, "This is the body of me, the [one] for you. This do in the remembrance of me."

25. Likewise also the cup, after the supping, saying, "This cup is the new covenant in the blood of me. This do, as often as you drink, in the remembrance of me."

26. For as often as you eat this bread and drink this cup, you proclaim the death of the Lord until he comes.

27. So then whoever eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord unworthily will be guilty of the body and the blood of the Lord.

28. But let a man examine himself, and thus let him eat from the bread and drink from the cup.

29. For the [one] eating and drinking, eats and drinks judgment to himself, not discerning the body.

30. On account of this many among you [are] weak and sick, and a number have fallen asleep.

31. But if we were discerning ourselves, we would not be judged.

32. But being judged by the Lord, we are disciplined, that we may not be condemned with the world.

33. So then, brothers of me, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.

34. If anyone is hungry, let him eat at home, that you may not come together for judgment. And the remaining [things] I will set in order when I come.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.3 — κεφαλή ("head"):** The meaning of "head" is intensely debated: "authority over" (traditional) or "source/origin" (egalitarian). Both meanings are attested in Greek. The argument of vv.8-9 (woman from man) supports "source," while v.10 ("authority on the head") suggests a hierarchy.

2. **v.10 — "authority upon the head":** ἐξουσίαν ἔχειν ἐπὶ τῆς κεφαλῆς — this is the most puzzling verse in the chapter. "Authority" (ἐξουσία) is the woman's own authority, not authority imposed upon her. "On account of the angels" may refer to angelic beings present in worship (cf. Qumran texts on worship propriety).

3. **vv.23-25 — The institution narrative:** This is the earliest written account of the Last Supper — predating the Gospels by at least a decade. Paul says he "received from the Lord" (παρέλαβον ἀπὸ τοῦ κυρίου) — either through direct revelation or through the chain of tradition traceable to Jesus.

4. **v.24 — Sinaiticus variant:** Sinaiticus reads "the [one] for you" (τὸ ὑπὲρ ὑμῶν) without adding "broken" (κλώμενον). This shorter reading is likely original.

5. **v.29 — "not discerning the body":** μὴ διακρίνων τὸ σῶμα — "body" here likely refers both to Christ's eucharistic body and to the church as Christ's body. Failing to "discern the body" means eating without recognizing the communal nature of the meal — eating selfishly while others go hungry.

— EMET, AI Translator
